program.name: NAB Graduate Program (2027 intake)
program.duration_text: NAB states that it is a 15-month program.
program.rotation_text: NAB states you will complete rotations in genuine roles and can do a four-week secondment in a business area of your choice.

eligibility.work_rights: You must be an Australian citizen, New Zealand citizen, or an Australian permanent resident at the time of applying. Temporary visa holders are not eligible.
eligibility.graduation_window: You must have completed an undergraduate or postgraduate degree in the past three years (2024, 2025 or 2026), or be due to complete it by the end of January 2027.
minimum_requirements: You must be an Australian citizen, New Zealand citizen, or an Australian permanent resident at the time of applying.
Temporary visa holders are not eligible.
You must have completed an undergraduate or postgraduate degree in the past three years (2024, 2025 or 2026), or be due to complete it by the end of January 2027.

stages.name: Step 1 - The online application
stages.details: You complete the online application form, share your academic and work experience, and nominate your chosen business area.

stages.name: Step 2 - Online assessment including a video interview
stages.details: You complete online assessments, and record responses to video interview questions.

stages.name: Step 3 - The engagement centre
stages.details: You take part in interactive activities and meet the NAB Early Careers team, current graduates, and business leaders.

stages.name: Step 4 - Offer
stages.details: If successful, NAB says they will call you a few days after your engagement centre.

exec_themes: Customer-centric strategy focused on improving key customer experiences (using feedback loops and “Must Win Battles”) to lift advocacy, retention and referrals.
Technology modernisation and digital transformation, including adoption of AI/GenAI, to simplify processes and improve customer and colleague experience.
Targeted growth priorities aimed at improving returns: grow business banking, drive deposit growth, and strengthen proprietary home lending.
Balance sheet strength and disciplined risk/provisioning to stay resilient while pursuing growth.
Supporting the transition to a net zero economy through climate strategy and environmental finance ambition.
commercial_context.profit_engine: NAB describes three priorities to drive stronger returns: (1) grow business banking (Business & Private Banking leadership and disciplined growth in Corporate & Institutional Banking), (2) drive deposit growth (deeper engagement, transaction accounts and payment solutions), and (3) strengthen proprietary home lending (increase lending through NAB-owned channels while managing margins/returns with discipline).
commercial_context.esg: NAB states it aims to reach net zero by 2050 across financed and facilitated emissions and its operations, and says its climate strategy was refreshed in 2025. It also sets an environmental finance ambition of $80 billion by 2030 and reports progress toward that ambition (with cumulative environmental finance reported as at 30 September 2025).
commercial_context.recent_pivot: NAB says it is implementing a refreshed customer-centric strategy, and is also leaning harder into AI/GenAI as part of its technology modernisation (including moving toward a business-led model focused on responsible AI, ethics and compliance).

notes: Key dates (open/close) and program start date are listed on NAB’s Graduate opportunities page.
Streams and locations are listed on the NAB Graduate Program 2027 job ad.
The job ad states the program is 15 months and mentions rotations and a four-week secondment.

notes: Headwinds were not stated clearly in the sources reviewed. The investor presentation focuses on priorities, strategy and performance, but does not provide a single, explicit list of macro/industry headwinds in the sections accessed.
The 2025 Annual Report PDF could not be fetched due to a tool retrieval error, so themes/headwinds that might be explicitly listed there were not directly extracted.

The climate/ESG summary is taken from NAB’s sustainability webpages (strategy/targets/ambitions). For a fuller, audit-style ESG view (metrics, governance, risk management), the NAB Climate Report PDF is referenced on the page but could not be fetched due to a tool retrieval error.
